videos.title: How clips ate the internet #Vergecast
videos.description:
Clips have taken over the internet. The Verge’s Mia Sato joins The Vergecast to break down how they work, why they’re everywhere, and what they’re doing to how we experience social media.

videos.title: You can find the Today I’m Toying With giveaway and official rules at theverge.com/titw
videos.description: Want to win robot toys, chargers, gaming gear and more? We’re giving away some of the actual gadgets we’ve shared here on YouTube in our Today I’m Toying With giveaway! The Verge’s strong ethics policy means our journalists don’t get to keep the gadgets or anything else a company sends. Usually we return or donate — but since Today I’m Toying With is all about sharing the joy of gadgets, we thought we’d share some actual gadgets, too! videos.description:

videos.title: Fitbit’s AI coach actually works | The Vergecast
videos.description: It's now surprisingly easy to watch most of a movie without ever trying to, or to spend hours with a podcast without ever playing an episode. In the burgeoning clip economy, everything is being cut into bite-sized pieces and being blasted around the internet hoping to land in your feeds. The Verge's Mia Sato explains the machinery of how all this works, and wonders what it means for our social media experience. After that, The Verge's Victoria Song joins to discuss the Fitbit Air, the new $99 Go

videos.title: The post-search Google era begins | The Vergecast
videos.description: Before we get into this week’s tech news, we have some corporate news to discuss, and some very exciting Vergecast news to share. (If you have questions about either one, hit us up: vergecast@theverge.com or 866-VERGE11!) Then, Nilay and David get back into the weeds on all things Google I/O, and in particular the ways AI is changing the Google Search experience. videos.title: Google I/O 2026 keynote in 35 minutes
videos.description: Google just wrapped one of its biggest keynotes of the year Google I/O 2026 and the company is all in on AI. Announcements included a new family of their Gemini 3.5 AI model which will add personalized AI in a whole suite of systems. Starting today, Gemini 3.5 Flash will be the default model for the Gemini app and AI Mode in Search and agent-first coding. Alongside Gemini 3.5, Google is launching Gemini Omni, a new AI generation tool which the company says can “create anything with any input” an

videos.title: Google I/O 2026 reactions | The Vergecast Livestream
videos.description:
Google I/O is upon us, which means Gemini season starts a few days early this year. We’ll go live after Google’s 1PM ET / 10AM PT keynote with reactions from The Verge’s senior AI reporter Hayden Field and executive editor Jake Kastrenakes.
